steps.title: Identify the data pattern: linear or exponential
steps.content: Examine the scatterplot visually. The points form a roughly straight line with no curvature or exponential growth. The data increases steadily and predictably as x increases, characteristic of linear (not exponential) behavior. This eliminates choices C and D, which are exponential models that would show curved growth.

steps.title: Determine the slope sign
steps.content: As x increases from left to right, y also increases throughout the plot. The slope is clearly positive. Choice B ($y = 582 - 1.2x$) has a negative slope, so it contradicts the upward trend in the data. Only choice A remains viable.

steps.title: Verify with a sample point from the graph
steps.content: Select a point near the middle of the plot: approximately (3000, 2900). Substitute into choice A: $$y = -582 + 1.2(3000) = -582 + 3600 = 3018$$ The predicted value of 3018 closely matches the observed y-value of approximately 2900, confirming the fit. That matches choice A — A is correct.
